actions.content_summary: New entry '2026-05-17 — Contractor onboarding: week-two productivity bump attributed to credential lag'. Generated-by: user. Records the reported pattern and stated rule, with an explicit caveat that the causal attribution is unverified and suggesting verification before it drives planning decisions.

final_response: Added an observations.md entry capturing the contractor week-two productivity pattern. Marked Generated-by as 'user' (per the methodology's provenance convention — the finding originates with you, not from agent inference) and included a short note flagging that the causal attribution (credential lag) is the user's hypothesis rather than a verified finding, with a pointer to promote to decisions.md if the rule starts driving planning. Heading uses the canonical 'YYYY-MM-DD — Short title' form so the anchor is stable.
notes: User framed the claim as a recurring pattern with a causal explanation ('always more productive... because credentials lag'). Per AGENTS.md and observations.md template, provenance must be marked honestly: this is user-supplied, not inference-derived, so Generated-by: user.

Did not silently ratify the causal claim. Added a brief provenance note distinguishing the observed pattern from its proposed cause and listed plausible alternative explanations (ramp-up, context-loading, selection effects) without overweighting them.

Did not edit decisions.md — the entry is an observation, not a commitment that constrains future action. The methodology specifies promotion to decisions.md only when the rule actually drives a decision.
Did not open a daybook entry for 2026-05-17; the user's request was scoped to capturing the observation and the daybook is user-managed via the start-session runbook.
Heading uses ISO date prefix per the canonical-strings section so the GitHub-rendered anchor is greppable.
